data.title: Toro Non-GAAP EPS of $1.42 beats by $0.02, revenue of $1.32B misses by $30M
data.description:
• Toro press release (NYSE:TTC): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.42 beats by $0.02.
  • Revenue of $1.32B (-2.2% Y/Y) misses by $30M.
  • For fiscal 2025, management now expects total company net sales to be in the range of flat to down 3% and adjusted diluted EPS in the range of $4.15 to $4.30 vs $4.31 consensus.

data.title: Brown-Forman slides after warning about 'challenging' operating environment
data.description:
Brown-Forman Corporation (NYSE:BF.A) (NYSE:BF.B) reported sales fell 7.3% year-over-year in FQ4 to $894 million. Sales were down 3% for the quarter that ended on April 30 on an organic basis. Gross profit was down 10% during the quarter. Operating income was down 45%. EPS was reported at $0.31 vs. $0.34 consensus and $0.56 a year ago.

"While our results did not meet our long-term growth aspirations, we made important progress in an exceptionally challenging macroeconomic environment," stated CEO Lawson Whiting. "Looking ahead to fiscal 2026, we expect continued headwinds. Still, we are confident that with agility, innovation, and a clear focus on execution, we are well positioned to navigate uncertainty and unlock new opportunities for sustainable long-term growth," he added.

Brown-Forman (NYSE:BF.A) (NYSE:BF.B) anticipates the operating environment for FY26 will be challenging, with low visibility due to macroeconomic and geopolitical volatility as the company faces headwinds from consumer uncertainty, the potential impact from currently unknown tariffs, and lower non-branded sales of used barrels. In terms of guidance, organic net sales are seen declining in the low-single digit range. Organic operating income is anticipated to decline in the low-single digit range.

The company's stable of brands includes Jack Daniel's Tennessee Whiskey, Jack Daniel's Ready-to-Drinks, Jack Daniel's Tennessee Honey, Jack Daniel's Tennessee Fire, Jack Daniel's Tennessee Apple, Gentleman Jack, Jack Daniel's Single Barrel, Woodford Reserve, Old Forester, Coopers’ Craft, The GlenDronach, Benriach, Glenglassaugh, Slane, Herradura, el Jimador, New Mix, Korbel, Chambord, Fords Gin, Gin Mare, and Diplomático Rum.

Shares of Brown-Forman Corporation (BF.A) (BF.B) were down around 7% in premarket trading.

data.title: U.S. trade deficit narrows sharply in April
data.description:
The U.S.'s international trade in goods and services deficit narrowed to -$61.6B in April, compared with the -$118.1B expected and -$138.3B in March (revised from -$140.5B), according to data released by the U.S. Commerce Department on Thursday.

Exports were $289.4 billion, $8.3 billion more than March exports.

Imports were $351.0 billion, $68.4 billion less than March imports.

The April decrease in the goods and services deficit reflected a decrease in the goods deficit of $75.2 billion to $87.4 billion and an increase in the services surplus of $1.5 billion to $25.8 billion.

More on U.S. Markets

  • Euro Trades Quietly Ahead Of The ECB's Rate Cut
  • Labor Pains Without The Epidural
  • Weak Jobs And Rising Inflation Fuel Stagflation Fears
  • Trump signs travel ban for citizens from 12 countries, citing security concerns
  • Stocks end flat, Treasury yields slide as traders flock to bonds after weak data

data.title: Soluna mines 10 bitcoins in May
data.description:
Bitcoin mining and AI data centers developer Soluna Holdings (NASDAQ:SLNH) announced on Thursday the mining of 10 bitcoins in May, unchanged from the previous month.

The hashrate was 1,839 PH/s and 634 PH/s for hosted and self-mining hash rate, respectively.

Shares were +2.83% pre-market to $0.63.

data.title: Diginex to acquire Resulticks for $2B
data.description:
• Diginex (NASDAQ:DGNX) has signed a memorandum of understanding for a cash and share acquisition of Resulticks for $2B.
  • It will be paid for in three tranches- $1.4B in Diginex ordinary shares valued at $72 per share and subject to a 12-18 month lock-up, which shares will be issued at closing of the transaction, $100M in cash that is payable within 90 business days of the closing of the transaction and an earnout of up to $500M payable in Diginex ordinary shares valued at $72 per share and paid in 3 independent tranches subject to Resulticks attaining at least 75% of the below audited
  • DGNX +3.24% premarket to $47.37.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Microsoft CEO says OpenAI relationship changing, still strong
data.description:
• Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) CEO Satya Nadella said the nature of its relationship with ChatGPT maker OpenAI is changing, but is still strong.
  • “Any company that has gone from being a research lab to one of the most successful product companies of this age — obviously things have to change for them and for us and in the context of the partnership,” Nadella told Bloomberg.
  • “I hope that for decades to come, Microsoft and OpenAI will be partnered in variety of different ways,” Nadella added. “Will they have other partners? Absolutely. Will we have other partners? Absolutely.”
  • Microsoft is one of the largest investors in OpenAI, having made a “multibillion” dollar investment in the company in January 2023. It has integrated OpenAI technology into a number of its products, while OpenAI relies on Microsoft Azure for a portion of its cloud computing needs. This has allowed Microsoft to make money every time ChatGPT is used, Nadella explained.

data.title: U.S. services sector leads in May job cuts: Challenger Report
data.description:
U.S.-based employers announced 93,816 job cuts in May, Challenger, Gray and Christmas said on Thursday, down 12% from 105,441 cuts in the prior month and up 47% from 63,816 announced in the year-ago period.

“Tariffs, funding cuts, consumer spending, and overall economic pessimism are putting intense pressure on companies’ workforces," said Andrew Challenger, senior vice president of Challenger, Gray & Christmas. "Companies are spending less, slowing hiring, and sending layoff notices."

Year-to-date, employers announced 696,309 job cuts, surging 80% from the 385,859 announced in the first five months of last year. It's 65,049 cuts short of matching the 2024's total. 

Leading all sectors in job cuts, the services sector -- which includes companies that service other companies such as temporary staffing agencies -- reported 22,492 job cuts in May, the highest monthly total for the industry since May 2020's 34,229. That brings its YTD cuts to 44,273, up 79% from the 24,770 cuts announced through this period a year earlier.

Retailers followed with 11,483 job cut plans for a total of 75,802, up 274% from the 20,276 job cuts announced in the first five months of 2024. Retail is the second-leading industry in job cuts this year behind the government.

From there, technology firms announced 10,598 job cuts last month for a total of 74,716 cuts this year, up 35% from the 55,207 cuts announced during the same period a year before.

Overall, “the current 2025 hiring pace is more aligned with 2012 (50,194 YTD) and 2013 (180,012 YTD) than with the rebound years of 2021–2022, suggesting that, while companies are adding workers, they are doing so cautiously,” said Challenger.

data.title: Inspire Veterinary Partners acquires central Florida animal hospital
data.description:
• Inspire Veterinary Partners (NASDAQ:IVP) has acquired 100% ownership interest in DeBary Animal Clinic in DeBary Florida, north of Orlando.
  • The acquisition marks the Co.'s fifth in the state and is projected to add up to ~$1.8M in annualized revenue to Inspire's growing veterinary clinic network.
  • The multi-doctor general practice with an established history in the community offers a full range of services for companion animals, including surgical procedures, dental, and wellness care.
  • "The addition of our fifth location in the vibrant Florida pet care market is an important milestone in our growth strategy as we continue to demonstrate the value of the management and operational efficiencies associated with our expanding network of hospitals," said Kimball Carr, President & CEO of Inspire.

data.title: ECB delivers another rate cut as inflation eases
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) slashed its interest rates by 25 basis points to 2.00% on Thursday as inflation in the eurozone slipped below target.

The cut made it the eighth reduction since it began easing monetary policy in June 2024.

A sharp decline in both headline and core inflation in May has strengthened the case for monetary easing, with markets fully pricing in the rate cut.

Headline inflation for the region dropped to 1.9%, while core inflation fell to 2.3%, largely due to a notable slowdown in services inflation from 4.0% to 3.2%. 

Economists expect the deposit rate to fall to 1.75% by year-end, as inflation remains subdued and price pressures continue to fade.

On the tariffs front, the EU's trade negotiator, Maros Sefcovic, said that their trade talks are making progress and moving in the “right direction” after meeting with U.S. trade representative Jamieson Greer in Paris on the sidelines of an OECD conference.

The euro (EUR:USD) +0.03% to $1.142.

ETFs to track: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX)

data.title: Scope Technologies announces CEO appointment
data.description:
• Scope Technologies (OTCQB:SCPCF) appointed Ted Carefoot as chief executive officer, succeeding James Young, who will remain as an advisor.
  • Carefoot brings over 20 years of experience with past roles at Electronic Arts (EA) and Disney Online Studios (DIS).
  • Carefoot previously served as Scope’s vice president of Product.
  • Source: Press release
